gaps.snippet: nodule std.swap;
gaps.reason: derived nodule path `std.swap` has segment(s) [swap] colliding with a Mycelium reserved word ŌĆö emitting them verbatim would fail to parse (`nodule std.swap;` tokenizes the colliding word as a keyword, not an identifier); each colliding segment is escaped with the `_kw` suffix (now `std.swap_kw`) rather than dropped, so distinct source files whose only differing segment is a reserved word (e.g. `l1.fuse` vs `l1.nodule`) stay distinguishable instead of both collapsing onto the same nodule path (G2/VR-5)

# [doc = " Validate that value `b` refines value `a` under the swap described by `cert` (M-210)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee tag: `Exact`** ŌĆö this is a verdict, not an approximation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - `Ok(strength)` ŌĆö validated at `strength` (derived from the cert's basis; VR-5)."] # [doc = " - `Err(CheckError::Refuted)` ŌĆö concrete counterexample (the swap is wrong)."] # [doc = " - `Err(CheckError::NotValidated)` ŌĆö TV-incompleteness or certificate mismatch; **not** a proof"] # [doc = "   of correctness, **never** a silent pass (RFC-0002 ┬¦2). The caller **must** take the enclosed"] # [doc = "   [`Fallback`] path (always [`Fallback::UseReference`])."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The relation is inferred from the certificate kind (`Bijective` ŌåÆ `Bijection`; `Bounded` ŌåÆ"] # [doc = " `BoundedSimilarity`). This surface delegates to the one M-210 checker in `mycelium-cert::check`"] # [doc = " ŌĆö it does not enlarge the trusted base (KC-3, C5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`CheckError`] when the verdict is not `Validated`."] pub fn check_swap (a : & Value , b : & Value , cert : & SwapCertificate ,) -> Result < GuaranteeStrength , CheckError > { use mycelium_numerics :: Certificate ; let (relation , claimed) = match cert { SwapCertificate :: Bijective { .. } => (RefinementRelation :: Bijection , Certificate :: exact ()) , SwapCertificate :: Bounded { bound , .. } => { let strength = bound . basis . strength () ; let claimed = match & bound . kind { BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm : _ } => { Certificate :: new (* eps , 0.0 , strength) . ok_or_else (| | { CheckError :: NotValidated { reason : NotValidatedReason :: CertificateMismatch { detail : "certificate ╬Ą is not a well-formed bound" . to_owned () , } , fallback : Fallback :: UseReference , } }) ? } BoundKind :: Probability { delta } => Certificate :: new (0.0 , * delta , strength) . ok_or_else (| | CheckError :: NotValidated { reason : NotValidatedReason :: CertificateMismatch { detail : "certificate ╬┤ is not a well-formed probability" . to_owned () , } , fallback : Fallback :: UseReference , }) ? , _ => { return Err (CheckError :: NotValidated { reason : NotValidatedReason :: Incomplete { detail : "bound kind not checkable at this checker version \

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One row of the guarantee matrix (RFC-0016 ┬¦4.5; swap.md ┬¦4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Encoded as data so tests can assert invariants rather than prose-only (RFC-0016 ┬¦4.5)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub struct MatrixRow { # [doc = " The exported operation's name."] pub op : & 'static str , # [doc = " The honest guarantee tag on the `Exact ŌŖÉ Proven ŌŖÉ Empirical ŌŖÉ Declared` lattice."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For bounded ops this is the *default* strength; the cert may reach `Proven` when the"] # [doc = " cited theorem's side-conditions check (see each op's documentation)."] pub guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , # [doc = " Whether the op is fallible (returns `Result`)."] pub fallible : bool , # [doc = " Whether the op emits or **is** a certificate artifact (EXPLAIN-able; C3)."] pub cert_carrying : bool , # [doc = " The certificate kind emitted (`\"Bijective\"`, `\"Bounded\"`, or `None` for non-swap ops)."] pub cert_kind : Option < & 'static str > , }
gaps.reason: struct `MatrixRow` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file ŌĆö emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The guarantee matrix for `std.swap` (RFC-0016 ┬¦4.5; swap.md ┬¦4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every invariant is asserted in the test suite via [`assert_matrix_invariants`] ŌĆö never"] # [doc = " prose-only (RFC-0016 ┬¦4.5, \"encoded as data, asserted in tests\")."] pub const GUARANTEE_MATRIX : & [MatrixRow] = & [MatrixRow { op : "bin_to_tern" , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallible : true , cert_carrying : true , cert_kind : Some ("Bijective") , } , MatrixRow { op : "tern_to_bin" , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallible : true , cert_carrying : true , cert_kind : Some ("Bijective") , } , MatrixRow { op : "f32_to_bf16" , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , fallible : true , cert_carrying : true , cert_kind : Some ("Bounded") , } , MatrixRow { op : "dense_to_vsa" , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , fallible : true , cert_carrying : true , cert_kind : Some ("Bounded") , } , MatrixRow { op : "vsa_to_dense" , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , fallible : true , cert_carrying : true , cert_kind : Some ("Bounded") , } , MatrixRow { op : "check_swap" , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallible : true , cert_carrying : false , cert_kind : None , } , MatrixRow { op : "explain" , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallible : false , cert_carrying : true , cert_kind : None , } ,] ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const GUARANTEE_MATRIX` ŌĆö co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Assert the structural invariants of the guarantee matrix ŌĆö called from tests."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This discharges the RFC-0016 ┬¦4.5 obligation: \"encoded as data, asserted in tests, never"] # [doc = " prose-only.\" Panics with a descriptive message on any violation."] pub fn assert_matrix_invariants () { for row in GUARANTEE_MATRIX { assert ! (! row . op . is_empty () , "matrix row has empty op name") ; if let Some (kind) = row . cert_kind { assert ! (kind == "Bijective" || kind == "Bounded" , "op {}: cert_kind must be 'Bijective' or 'Bounded', got {kind:?}" , row . op) ; } if row . cert_kind == Some ("Bijective") { assert_eq ! (row . guarantee , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , "op {}: Bijective cert implies Exact guarantee (RFC-0002 ┬¦4)" , row . op) ; } if row . cert_kind == Some ("Bounded") { assert_ne ! (row . guarantee , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , "op {}: Bounded cert cannot be Exact (Exact means no bound; M-I1)" , row . op) ; } if ! row . fallible && row . cert_kind . is_some () { panic ! ("op {}: non-fallible op should not emit a swap cert_kind" , row . op) ; } } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use mycelium_core :: { operation_hash , Payload , ScalarKind } ; # [doc = " A canonical policy hash used across all tests ŌĆö stands in for a real RFC-0005 policy."] fn test_policy () -> PolicyRef { operation_hash ("test.policy.std_swap.v1") } # [doc = " Build a `Binary{n}` value from a signed integer."] fn make_binary (value : i64 , width : u32) -> Value { use mycelium_core :: { binary , GuaranteeStrength , Meta , Provenance } ; let bits = binary :: int_to_bits (value , width) . unwrap_or_else (| | panic ! ("value {value} does not fit in {width} bits")) ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Root , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , None , None , None , None ,) . expect ("meta is well-formed") ; Value :: new (Repr :: Binary { width } , Payload :: Bits (bits) , meta) . expect ("binary value is well-formed") } # [doc = " Build a `Ternary{m}` value from a signed integer."] fn make_ternary (value : i64 , trits : u32) -> Value { use mycelium_core :: { GuaranteeStrength , Meta , Provenance } ; let trit_vec = mycelium_core :: ternary :: int_to_trits (value , trits) . unwrap_or_else (| | panic ! ("value {value} does not fit in {trits} trits")) ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Root , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , None , None , None , None ,) . expect ("meta is well-formed") ; Value :: new (Repr :: Ternary { trits } , Payload :: Trits (trit_vec) , meta) . expect ("ternary value is well-formed") } # [doc = " Build a `Dense{n, F32}` value from a slice of f64 scalars."] fn make_dense_f32 (xs : & [f64]) -> Value { use mycelium_core :: { GuaranteeStrength , Meta , Provenance } ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Root , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , None , None , None , None ,) . expect ("meta is well-formed") ; Value :: new (Repr :: Dense { dim : xs . len () as u32 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F32 , } , Payload :: Scalars (xs . to_vec ()) , meta ,) . expect ("dense F32 value is well-formed") } # [doc = " The guarantee matrix is internally consistent (RFC-0016 ┬¦4.5)."] # [doc = " Mutation witness: set cert_kind=Some(\"Bijective\") on f32_to_bf16 ŌåÆ assertion fires."] # [test] fn guarantee_matrix_invariants_hold () { assert_matrix_invariants () ; } # [doc = " All expected ops appear in the matrix exactly once."] # [doc = " Mutation witness: remove a row from GUARANTEE_MATRIX ŌåÆ count == 0."] # [test] fn matrix_contains_all_ops_exactly_once () { let expected = ["bin_to_tern" , "tern_to_bin" , "f32_to_bf16" , "dense_to_vsa" , "vsa_to_dense" , "check_swap" , "explain" ,] ; for op in & expected { let count = GUARANTEE_MATRIX . iter () . filter (| r | r . op == * op) . count () ; assert_eq ! (count , 1 , "op '{op}' must appear exactly once in the matrix") ; } } # [doc = " Bijective-cert ops are tagged Exact; Bounded-cert ops are not Exact."] # [test] fn matrix_tag_cert_kind_consistency () { for row in GUARANTEE_MATRIX { match row . cert_kind { Some ("Bijective") => assert_eq ! (row . guarantee , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , "op {}: Bijective implies Exact" , row . op) , Some ("Bounded") => assert_ne ! (row . guarantee , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , "op {}: Bounded cannot be Exact" , row . op) , _ => { } } } } # [doc = " `dec(enc x) == Ok(x)` for every value in the n=8 corpus on the legal (8, 6) pair."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the property test for the `LosslessWithinRange` guarantee (RFC-0002 ┬¦4)."] # [doc = " Mutation witness: change trits to 5 (illegal pair) ŌåÆ enc returns Err(IllegalPair)."] # [test] fn bin_tern_round_trip_full_n8_corpus () { let policy = test_policy () ; let (n , m) = (8u32 , 6u32) ; assert !
